forecast-period.text: A cold front over eastern districts will move to the east of SA overnight. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the early morning then decreasing to 20 to 25 knots in the late evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Vincent to Brighton during the morning.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port Vincent to Brighton in the morning and afternoon.
West to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the late morning.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots during the evening.
